title: Extensions not injected into webview tabs (partition mismatch)
description: Extensions load into session.defaultSession but webview tags use partition 'persist:riced', so content scripts never inject into tab content. Fix: load extensions from the same partition session the webviews use.
close_reason: Extensions loaded into session.fromPartition('persist:riced-chromium') instead of defaultSession. Webview partition aligned to same value so content scripts inject into tab webviews.

title: Fix startup crash in tab order persistence
description:
Renderer PaperTM calls storage.loadTabOrder/saveTabOrder during tabs updates, but those IPC methods are not exposed by preload. In production this throws on the first tabs:updated event, preventing tab rendering and leaving the app stuck on the splash/new-tab screen.

Add the missing IPC/preload plumbing (or guard the calls) so tabs can render and navigation works again.
close_reason: Added tab-order IPC/preload plumbing and guarded PaperTM persistence lookup; verified with full test suite.

title: Canvas pages — wire real data via IPC
description:
Canvas pages (right sidebar, canvas-pages.js) currently use window._phase7History / window._phase7Bookmarks globals from HTML data attributes.

Replace with actual IPC calls to storage module:
- **history** → ipcRenderer.invoke("storage:get", "history")
- **bookmarks** → ipcRenderer.invoke("storage:get", "bookmarks")
- Support real-time updates via IPC events on data change
- Handle empty/loading/error states in canvas pages

See handoff Step 1. Phase 7 UI: src/renderer/js/right-sidebar.js, src/renderer/js/canvas-pages.js

title: Miniapps sandboxing — iframe/webview isolation
description:
Miniapps (calculator, etc.) currently render inline HTML in the page context. Plan requires sandboxed iframes or webviews for security isolation.

Work needed:
1. Audit all miniapps for danger (eval replaced, but still same-origin)
2. Design sandbox strategy: iframe with srcdoc vs webview partition
3. Implement sandbox for new miniapps
4. Migrate existing miniapps incrementally
5. Verify CSP still holds under sandbox

Plan ref: src/renderer/feature-platform/PLAN.md
See handoff for eval() fix already applied.

title: Fix PR #4 review issues: symlink scan, partition session, trust concurrency, ai perm, eval, shortcuts
description:
Fixed 6 issues found during review of PR #4 (browser-native-platform branch):

1. scanExtensions: restored isSymbolicLink() check + try/catch for broken symlinks (regression from PR)
2. extension-loader: restored session.fromPartition('persist:riced-chromium') for extension loading (PR had reverted to defaultSession, breaking content script injection)
3. papertm.js: restored webview partition to 'persist:riced-chromium' (PR had 'persist:riced')
4. TrustManager.request(): fixed concurrent request bug where second caller's promise was never resolved/rejected
5. app.js: added ai::complete to registerDefaults (AIClient.enable() would throw without it)
6. miniapps/index.js: replaced eval() in calculator with safe recursive-descent expression parser (CSP compliance)
7. app.js + index.html: aligned Shell shortcut to Ctrl+Shift+S (was Ctrl+Shift+X, conflicted with bash canvas page)

All 396 tests pass.
close_reason: Committed and pushed all 6 fixes to browser-native-platform branch. 396 tests pass.

close_reason: Added ModeManager sync and active indicator to _toggleMiniapps()

title: TrustManager permissions not granted at boot — only shell::execute registered, all feature-platform buttons silently fail

Actual permissions needed: editor::write, inspector::inspectDom, actions::execute, data::scrape, workflows::execute

Fix: Add all defaults in app.js init() TrustManager.registerDefaults() call.

title: CanvasPages.close() method missing — only init/expose, close delegated to RightSidebar

canvas-pages.js only exposes init and open. No close method. RightSidebar.closeCanvas() exists. PHASE7.md spec says CanvasPages should have close().

Fix: Add close() to CanvasPages public API that calls RightSidebar.closeCanvas().

title: Refactor browser shell for low-spec AI browser
description:
## Problem Statement
Need highly capable AI browser that runs well on low-spec hardware and can grow into browser shell for host-machine communication. Current app is good browser prototype, but shell bridge, terminal streaming, safety policy, and low-spec runtime shaping are not yet designed as one coherent path. Need preserve seamless feel, keep monolith, and avoid framework churn unless it clearly helps.

## Solution
Keep Electron for now. It already matches current stack, host-process needs, and low-spec practicality better than a framework swap. Add a browser-shell layer that exposes two-way, allowlisted host command execution with streamed terminal output into the renderer. Keep current browser UX intact, add shell surface as a first-class tool, and tune runtime for weak hardware. Leave cloud OS expansion and paper-style tab manager for later layers.

## Commits
1. Define shell contract and policy shape with no runtime behavior change.
2. Add command normalization and allowlist checks in main process.
3. Add a streaming command runner using a terminal-style process bridge, with stdout/stderr/input/exit events.
4. Expose shell IPC through preload with a minimal API, but no UI yet.
5. Add renderer shell surface scaffold hidden by default.
6. Wire open/close/focus/clear controls and stream output into the browser shell UI.
7. Add graceful stop/restart, backpressure, and session cleanup.
8. Add low-spec runtime tuning and memory/CPU guardrails without changing core navigation flow.
9. Add tests for policy, lifecycle, and IPC contract behavior.
10. Add smoke coverage for shell open, command stream, and tab/browser coexistence.
11. Update docs for shell behavior, safety model, and intended future expansion.

## Decision Document
- Electron stays for now.
- Monolith stays; no module-splitting refactor as the primary goal.
- Shell is two-way but allowlisted by default.
- Execution is local only.
- Streaming terminal behavior is required; one-shot command output is not enough.
- Existing browser shell, vertical tabs, sidecar, and extension support stay conceptually intact.
- Paper-style tab manager stays as later work, not this refactor.
- Cloud OS expansion stays as roadmap, not this step.
- Safety policy must live near command execution, not in renderer-only code.

## Testing Decisions
- Test external behavior only.
- Unit test command normalization, allowlist decisions, and shell lifecycle state.
- Unit test renderer state updates for shell stream handling.
- Smoke test tab switching plus shell open/close plus streamed output in one session.
- Reuse existing Jest style as prior art.
- Keep tests focused on visible browser-shell behavior, not internal implementation details.

## Out of Scope
- Cloud OS implementation.
- PaperWM-style tab manager implementation.
- Framework migration to CEF.
- General unrestricted shell access with no allowlist.
- Full AI backend wiring.
- Major visual redesign.
- Large-scale module split or package restructuring.

## Further Notes
Target machine is low-spec: 2-core Celeron 1000M, 3.7 GiB RAM, heavy swap use. Electron is the pragmatic choice right now because it reduces rewrite risk and supports host bridge needs without a second framework migration.
close_reason: Implemented browser shell contract, allowlisted terminal bridge, renderer shell panel, and tests.

title: Fix tab state persistence/switching
description: Tabs lose state when switching and load behavior is flaky. Likely cause: renderer keeps local URL/title state, but main tabManager never receives navigation updates, so broadcasts can overwrite newer renderer state with stale values on switch/reload.

title: HTML-in-Canvas native rendering via ctx.drawElementImage
description:
Phase 7 plan requires Electron 38+ for ctx.drawElementImage() to render HTML elements as canvas textures natively.

Current: canvas pages render as DOM overlays (divs positioned over canvas).
Target: native canvas rendering via drawElementImage for better performance and compositing.

Blockers:
- Electron 42 has the API available
- Need to refactor canvas-pages.js to use native canvas rendering instead of DOM overlays
- HTML-in-Canvas API docs: docs/html-in-canvas-api.md
- Need to verify drawElementImage works with webview/iframe content

Deferred — no immediate timeline.

title: PaperTM: Tab groups
description:
Implement tab groups: nest related tabs in the strip.

**What to build:**
- Data model: group ID, title, collapsed state, member tabs
- Group header in tab strip
- Expand/collapse toggle
- Nested tabs with indentation
- Context menus for group actions
- Persistence

**Non-goals:**
- Named groups → follow-up
- Group-level context menu → follow-up
- Multi-level nesting → follow-up

First thin slice: renderable group with header, expand/collapse button, and nested tabs (static data).

title: PaperTM: Minimap overview
description:
Create minimap view: condensed overview of all tabs in strip.

**What to build:**
- Minimap UI component: thumbnail list or condensed strip
- Shows all tabs with titles/favicons
- Current active tab indicator
- Click jumps to corresponding tab
- Scroll position synchronized with main strip
- Floating or sidebar-integrated
- Configurable size/opacity/position

**Non-goals:**
- Live preview → follow-up
- Grouped minimap for tab hierarchy → Phase 4
- Animated transitions → follow-up

Deliverable: functional minimap attached to sidebar that shows all tabs and allows jumping between them.

title: PaperTM: Scroll-to-switch
description:
Implement scroll-to-switch: aggressive scroll past threshold switches active tab.

**What to build:**
- Scroll velocity/position detection on tab-list
- Configurable threshold (overscroll past threshold = switch)
- Switch active tab when threshold crossed (direction-aware)
- Optional scroll animation to new active tab
- Settings UI for adjusting threshold/sensitivity

**Non-goals:**
- Two-finger scroll → follow-up
- Momentum-based detection → follow-up

Deliverable: thin slice that switches active tab on overscroll with clear visual and audible feedback (e.g., smooth scroll to active tab).

title: PaperTM: Drag to reorder tabs
description:
Implement drag-and-drop reordering for tabs in the scrollable strip. 

**What to build:**
- Draggable tab UI with drag handle / whole-tab drag
- Visual feedback while dragging (target indicator, ghost tab)
- Update DOM order on drop
- Update tab order state in PaperTM
- Persist order via storage
- Restore order on startup

**Non-goals:**
- Mouse-free reordering (keyboard) → follow-up
- Animation polish → follow-up
- Accessibility announcements → follow-up

Issues from this should include a small but functional slice that shows tabs moving with visual feedback and persisted order.

title: Multi-profile support
description:
Add multi-profile support so users can create/manage separate browser profiles (bookmarks, history, extensions, settings isolated per profile).

Scope:
- Profile manager UI in settings
- Profile data isolation in storage layer
- Profile-specific session partitions
- Profile switching without restart
- Extension sets per profile

See handoff handoffs/handoff-20260609-owl-205026.md for initial context.

title: Bookmark history persistence
description: Add bookmark button, dialog, history search, import export.

title: Decide extension execution/isolation model
description: Need a decision on whether extensions should run in separate isolates/processes with shared network/session state, or stay directly in Chromium extension context. Clarify what Electron supports for session sharing, isolation, and extension APIs before implementation.
notes: Recommendation: direct (session.defaultSession). Doc in docs/extension-model.md. Git 915ac68.

title: Add shortcut dropdown on splash screen
description: Ctrl+L and other shortcuts are inconsistent when focus is inside webviews. Add a dropdown under speed dial on the new tab/splash screen showing available keyboard shortcuts, so users can discover them without relying on in-page capture.
close_reason: Synced tab metadata from renderer to main, registered webview shortcuts, and added splash shortcut dropdown.

title: Print dialog — integrate Electron webContents.print() with custom UI. Toolbar button + Ctrl+P shortcut. Preview, page range, orientation, scale, destination.
PDF viewer
description: Native PDF viewer tab page. Opens PDF files in-browser instead of downloading. Uses Electron's PDF viewer capabilities.
acceptance_criteria: PDF opens in new tab, navigation (prev/next/zoom), download button, print button
close_reason: PDF viewer with page-by-page nav, zoom, download, print. Uses pdfjs-dist for rendering, custom surfboard://pdf internal page. 6 new unit tests.

value: P1/P2 test beads deferred by user: riced-chromium-48m, riced-chromium-4yy, riced-chromium-7cc, riced-chromium-8co, riced-chromium-43i, riced-chromium-i1c, riced-chromium-k07. P0 tests (23v, s23) written and pushed.

Phase 7 button issue: root cause was zero error boundaries in app.js init() + missing #right-sidebar-toggle handler. Fixed with try/catch per section, ?. on listeners, F12 DevTools, positive logging. BLOCKED on user confirming [init] rsidebar buttons attached: 8/8 in console. If 8/8 but buttons dead = CSS/caching. If missing = DOM mismatch.
Prefer archive-folder moves over force overwrite/delete in this repo; do not use destructive cp/rm to alter files.
